description: Lock the 1810 mid-career staff-college tier to its professional military-education lineage without laundering later rail or telegraph technologies into founding prerequisites.

checks.reason: Public railways postdate the 1810 staff-college anchor and became later planning subjects rather than a founding condition.
Electric telegraphy postdates the scoped institution and cannot enable its initial establishment.
Earlier military technical schools represent the documented institutional foundation from which the staff-college tier developed.
Technical military schools precede and inform staff colleges without constituting a universal engineering requirement.
Logistical complexity remains relevant professional knowledge within higher command and staff education.
Logistics is contextual curriculum knowledge, not a capability required to create the educational institution.
The scoped mid-career tier is anchored to Prussia's General War School, established in 1810.
